reviews.review_text:
As a small chud I cannot recommend this game. the blocks are WAY too high for my frail stature to hit, and the controllers are far too heavy. I have tried tirelessly to reach my goal of hitting just ONE block and it still eludes me . I know i have 3000 hours but i will be reaching out to steam support for a full refund.

sincerely,
The Small Chud Gaming Community
Add 30 dollars to your steam wallet and then pirate this so that you support Valve and not Meta, good game though

You can't be subjected to stutters in a rhythm game. My PC should have ZERO issues running this game. I've disabled, tweaked and troubleshot the issue as much as I can and it's still borderline unplayable due to stutters.

[This review comes from a longtime quest player, so most of my hours are there]

This game on itself is just, fine.
You kinda need to play modded or else the 30 bucks you spent on this game will never be worth it
Meta bought the game and ruined it for everyone,
focusing on DLC packs instead of the free OST packs and there's no variety with the OST packs 
the same three or four artists with the same sound all throughout

Beat saber peaked in OST 5, most songs, most variety
feels like new songs also don't care about people who want to have fun, 
expert+ became less about fun but skill, which I don't mind obviously but newer maps feel like you have to twist your bones to hit notes, like the difficulty is intended for those that DO play modded

meanwhile, DLC maps get the songs people DO want and with actual good, fun mapping 
I just can't get into the flow with OST maps

Modded adds alot of QoL features and makes the game actually enjoyable, you get actual songs that you want
and the option to do ranked (featuring a pp system like osu)

I'm genuinely disappointed about the state of the game right now, however it's still a lot of fun modded

and ever since meta bought the game, the game has been horribly mistreated on Steam - this game hasn't been on sale in YEARS - it only went on sale ONCE, meanwhile the game constantly goes on sale on the horizon platform, and sometimes even given out for free (if you get the 30 dollars from their referral program)

they've constantly made it harder to mod the game on the quest, you NEED a computer to mod it now.

TL;DR
10/10 game, meta ruined the main game, play modded.

P.S. OST 8 sucks, and the new extra 3 songs are just sad - 8th year anniversary and we only get scraps.
Screw you, Meta.
GREAT workout game lots of fun too i would recomend %100 and runs great on my pc too

Beat Saber is a fantastic game, if you figure out how to install mods.
This is one of those VR games that can almost justify buying a headset on its own. In many ways, it's a VR classic. The concept is simple: hit blocks to the rhythm of music. But it works incredibly well because the gameplay feels perfectly suited to VR. You're moving your arms, following the beat, and physically interacting with the music instead of just pressing buttons. It's easy to learn and consistently fun to play.
The biggest problem is the developers' greed. There is no built in way to browse and download custom songs, so you're expected to either buy DLC packs separately or instal mods. Seriously, it’s the same level of supervillainous greed as Paradox Interactive with their Stellaris. The DLC library is huge, but there is no guarantee it will contain the music you're actually interested in.
Thankfully, mods solve this problem and make the game significantly better. The only downside is that setting them up can be more complicated than it should be, especially for a VR game.
Despite that, Beat Saber remains one of the best VR games available and an easy recommendation for anyone with a headset.

I honestly really love the classic Beat Saber, and once I got the Steam version for it (since I was in the native Quest app), it has been going pretty great ever since. Would definitely recommend to anyone on VR.
absolutely amazing game when modded but like just dont injur ur wrists other than that best game ive ever played fire emoji

Beat Saber is easily one of the best VR games out there with super fun and satisfying gameplay that never gets old. My only real complaint is that the base song selection feels limited and there are a lot of paid song packs, which can get expensive, but thankfully the modding community completely fixes that with custom songs and tons of extra content that make the game even more amazing overall.
if you want a workout and love jamming out, this is def a fun game to play, especially with mods to play community made songs for variety.
Great fun and a good workout. Highly recommended if you enjoy rhythm games. Grab the Prodigy music pack.

i dont play this game a lot but it's so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ satisfying cutting ♥♥♥♥. i have played a bunch of rhythm games in the past and like?? it's kinda hard to cut something on the beat of the song? but the timing windows aren't that bad so like it doesn't end up mattering that much i think. if you enjoy rhythm games then you will probably enjoy this. my mom really enjoyed this and she's not even a rhythm gamer or a frequent vr user

Easily the only VR game needed to justify a headset purchase, I bought a 2nd hand quest 2 just for this.
Rythm gamer's home workout wet dream basically, at home, active, fun. Just... use BSmanager and dont bother with the latest version, go grab your songs off beatsaver and you can play any song you want.... the official list is kinda assed tbh

I love this game, I played this on my HTC Vive, I love how you can mod with it too that is so easy like custom sabers, avatars, custom platforms, ETC. This game is worth getting If you like to play songs and mod it. Beat Saber is better when you have mods. Default beat saber without mods is still good and all but modding would be better for Beat Saber.
A must have if you have VR. Base game is solid. Mods make it even better. Cheers to the song mappers out there making magic

One of the few "physical" activity that I thoroughly enjoy and remain consistent in doing it every day. Lost 40 pounds (Of course I cut down on my diet as well but that's beside the point). Need I say more?
Litrlaly the most fun i have had in vr in like forever idk how to explain it just buy so worth.. also DEFS get mods, use bs-manager it makes it so much more intresting

Back when this came out it was a masterpiece of a game.
Now, 6 years on, it's a bottomless pit of absurdly priced "DLC" (aka song packs).
There are mods that make it better, for example allowing you to download additional songs & user created levels. But those mods frequently get broken when the game is updated to a new version (I assume this is purposeful by the devs), so if you go down this route I recommend you google how to install an older (mod friendly) version of the game and how to set things up so that you stay on that version. You lose out on "updates" but those are almost exclusively overpriced music packs so you're not really missing out.

I completely forgot that the last version of this game is borked on linux, so I bought The Prodigy DLC just to not being able to launch it! Why do I have to play 1.40.8 version? Fix your game

UPD: The issue is related to WayVR overlay. When I disabled WayVR, everything got fixed, 1.44.0 works flawlessly with all codecs I've tested, while WayVR works only with H265. Need to dig deeper, maybe I'll update the review later, but now it seems stupid for me to leave a negative review. In defense of my stupidness I'll say that Beat Saber is the only borked game, any other game work flawlessly. VR on linux can be hard sometimes

Too many hit detection issues for any otherwise great game.

It's hard to get into a flow when I slice clean through a block that's right in front of my face, and it registers a miss.
Отличная соковыжималка, Больше часа пока осилить не смог (мб если только на легком уровне сложности играть то можно и больше, но там не интересно)

Beat Saber is one of the only reasons I even have a VR, it was one of the first games I played on PSVR before I got a Quest 2. I don't use it much anymore, but anytime I do, I always play a few songs. Mods, Custom Songs, Multiplayer, always something to have fun with
i like the mods on this game. and i like playing songs from Slayer and Metallica

Spent all of my remaining steam gift card money for this expensive game. Great game it is worth the 44 bucks, gameplay is challenging, very addictive, and cool modifiers and songs. Probably one of the top vr gamers out there.

I Played This On my VR, Before I Got My Gaming PC, And i Loved It The First time I Played It.
I 100% Recommend This Game.
mod the game and boom you created the best VR rhythm game

Can't really say I recommend it.

Notes keep appearing at my wrists, making it near impossible to hit them. 
I altered the room Z offset and stood back, same issue.
Decently big guardian space, well lit room.

On top of that it constantly misses obvious hits, even with a 3080 on wifi 6 and ~4-10ms latency on encode, network, gpu, decode. 

Maybe in a few years the tech will be good enough. Doesn't seem like it is right now.
HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE HIT CUBE
This game makes me feel cool even though I probably look like those inflatable guys at those dealerships
After the last update, the cubes pass thru the laser swords and say that I didn't hit the cube. I even tried slowing down time, but nothing helps; the cubes don't see the laser swords
fun game if your looking to hit things and jam out to music
man i love it when the editor crashes halfway through my w charting session
I think this game is the first game that can come up to mind when you hear VR, It doesn't get boring at all
getting me moving like crazy like bro after 20 minutes im sweating my f-u-c-k-i-n-g balls off
I really like to play it, i feel like you could play it instead of working out, because your moving so much. But i would want it to have more kpop songs from other groups, not just BTS.

fun game, as long as you know how to mod the game, consider beatleader + scoresaber too if you want to be in a competitive community and climb leaderboards
- game feels very vanilla without mods but unless you REALLY like the songs, i personally don't recommend buying song packs since they're like $2 (usd) a song, would probably suggest adding custom songs instead 
- the game also quite literally never goes on sale (except for that one time it was $20, but this was shortly after its launch)

ig on the bright side you can at least have a good workout from beat saber from doing fitbeat-styled charts, you also don't need a super beefy computer to run the game, you just need a pc that can run vr

[spoiler] it's been nearly half a decade and they STILL haven't released custom sabers yet [/spoiler]
